back: T-score <= -2.5 at lumbar spine, total hip, or femoral neck (33% radius in specific cases).

front: WHO T-score range for osteopenia (low bone mass)
back: Between -1.0 and -2.5.
T-score <= -2.5 PLUS one or more fragility fractures.
LSC = 2.77 × precision error. Derivation: 1.96 × sqrt(2).
30 patients scanned twice OR 15 patients scanned three times, with repositioning. Both yield 30 degrees of freedom.

front: ISCD minimum precision at lumbar spine, total hip, femoral neck
back: Spine 1.9%, total hip 1.8%, femoral neck 2.5%. LSCs: 5.3%, 5.0%, 6.9%.
BMD: 3 decimals. Area and BMC: 2 decimals. T-score and Z-score: 1 decimal. Percent: integer.
BMD = BMC / Area, in g/cm². Areal (2D projection).
(Patient BMD - Young-adult mean BMD) / Young-adult SD. Reference: white female age 20-29.
(Patient BMD - Age-matched mean) / Age-matched SD. Z <= -2.0 is 'below expected range for age.'

front: Bone Mass Measurement Act coverage interval
back: Medicare covers central DXA every 23 months (with 5 qualifying conditions and exceptions).
SPA: Iodine-125 (single energy). DPA: Gadolinium-153 (dual energy, 44 and 100 keV).
75 scored + 30 pilot = 105 items. 1h45m answering. Pass = 75 scaled score (~70% correct). 2024 first-time pass rate 68.8%.
80% cortical, 20% trabecular. Trabecular has higher turnover, responds earlier to therapy.

front: Site composition: spine, 33% radius, ultra-distal radius
back: Vertebral bodies ~66% trabecular. 33% radius ~95% cortical. Ultra-distal radius mostly trabecular.
Blasts build (mesenchymal). Clasts carve (hematopoietic). Cytes = mechanosensors in lacunae (make sclerostin, regulate RANKL).
Activation, Resorption (2-3 wk), Reversal, Formation (~3 mo), Mineralization (months).
Intramembranous: flat skull bones, clavicle, direct from mesenchyme. Endochondral: long bones, via cartilage template.
~30% organic (90% type I collagen), ~70% inorganic (hydroxyapatite calcium phosphate).
Structural unit of CORTICAL bone. Concentric lamellae around central canal. Volkmann canals connect osteons.
~80-90% acquired by age 18; peak in late 20s. Girls accrue 40-50% of lifetime mass in early teen years.
Type 1 (postmenopausal): trabecular loss, vertebral/distal forearm fractures. Type 2 (senile): cortical+trabecular, hip/vertebral, both sexes >70.
Glucocorticoids (most common iatrogenic), hyperparathyroidism, hyperthyroidism, hypogonadism, malabsorption, CKD, myeloma, RA.

front: Preferred DXA site in primary hyperparathyroidism
back: 33% radius because primary hyperparathyroidism preferentially damages cortical bone.
P1NP (preferred per IOF/IFCC), bone-specific alkaline phosphatase, osteocalcin.
Serum CTX (preferred), urinary/serum NTX, deoxypyridinoline.
Calcium, 25-OH vitamin D (target >=30 ng/mL), PTH, ALP, TSH, sex hormones as indicated, 24hr urine calcium.
Osteomalacia: defective mineralization, usually low vit D. Soft bone, pseudofractures. Osteoporosis: normal mineralization, reduced quantity.
Under 50 and men 50-70: 1000 mg. Women 51+ and men 71+: 1200 mg.
Adults <70: 600 IU. 71+: 800 IU. BHOF: 800-1000 IU supplemental for 50+. Serum 25(OH)D target >=30 ng/mL.
Inhibit farnesyl pyrophosphate synthase, disabling osteoclasts. Bind hydroxyapatite for years (drug holiday possible).
Alendronate 70 mg PO weekly. Risedronate 35 mg weekly or 150 mg monthly. Ibandronate 150 mg PO monthly or 3 mg IV quarterly. Zoledronic acid 5 mg IV yearly.
Anti-RANKL mAb, 60 mg SC q6mo. Fully reversible. Discontinuation causes rebound fractures. Must transition to bisphosphonate.
PTH 1-34 analog, 20 µg SC daily. ANABOLIC. Max 2 years. Must follow with antiresorptive.
Anti-sclerostin mAb, 210 mg SC monthly × 12 months. Dual action. Boxed CV warning.
SERM, 60 mg PO daily. Reduces vertebral fracture AND invasive breast cancer. VTE risk.
T-score <= -2.5; OR low bone mass + FRAX MOF >=20% or hip >=3%; OR hip/vertebral fragility fracture.
After 5 years oral OR 3 years IV in patients no longer at high risk. NOT for denosumab or anabolics.

front: Pencil vs fan vs narrow-fan beam
back: Pencil: single detector, slow, no magnification. Fan: array, fast, magnification. Narrow fan (GE Lunar): multi-pass, near-pencil geometry.

front: Hologic vs GE Lunar energy method
back: Hologic: kVp switching (100/140 kV) + internal calibration wheel. GE Lunar: K-edge filter + external calibration.
Accuracy = close to truth. Precision = reproducible. Trueness = systematic agreement with reference. Serial monitoring needs precision.
1.5% rule: Shewhart limits at ±1.5% of baseline. ISCD: weekly minimum; manufacturers: daily.
One point beyond ±3 SD; two consecutive beyond ±2 SD same side; four consecutive beyond ±1 SD same side; ten consecutive same side of mean.
10 phantom scans before and after. Greater than 1% difference triggers service.
30 patients once on old, twice on new within 60 days. One-time intersystem LSC only.
Spine phantom on both scanners on 20 different days. Greater than 0.5% difference triggers service.
Converts manufacturer BMD to common scale. NOT for serial monitoring across manufacturers.
Central DXA 1-15 µSv per scan. Less than 1 day of natural background (~8 µSv/day, 3000/year).
Chest x-ray 20-100 µSv. Mammogram 400 µSv. Tc bone scan ~3000 µSv. CT abdomen 8000-10000 µSv.
IV iodinated contrast 72hr. Barium 7-14d. Nuclear med 10 half-lives (Tc-99m 72hr). Oral iodine 3-7d.
ABSOLUTE contraindication to body composition. Strong relative to BMD. Defer unless clear benefit.

front: Minimum operator distance from scan table
back: 1 m (3 ft). Scatter at 1 m: pencil ~0.01 µSv/hr, fan ~5 µSv/hr.
Time, Distance, Shielding.
20 mSv/year avg over 5 years, 50 mSv single-year max. Declared pregnant: 1 mGy conceptus.

front: Prohibited terms in pediatric DXA reports
back: T-scores: NEVER. 'Osteopenia': NEVER. 'Osteoporosis': only with clinically significant fracture history.
Vertebral compression fracture alone = diagnosis. OR Z-score <= -2.0 PLUS 2+ long-bone fx by 10 or 3+ by 19.
PA spine L1-L4 and TBLH. Other: 33% radius (ambulatory), lateral distal femur (non-ambulatory).

front: Preferred pediatric language for low Z-score
back: 'Low bone mineral mass or bone mineral density for chronologic age' when Z-score <= -2.0.
Spine: BMAD or height-for-age Z-score. TBLH: height-for-age Z-score. CHOP calculator is the BHOF-referenced tool.
Ages 40-90. Outputs 10-yr MOF and 10-yr hip fracture probability. MOF = vertebral + hip + forearm + proximal humerus.
Femoral neck BMD ONLY. Not total hip, not lumbar spine. Entering BMD auto-inactivates secondary osteoporosis flag.
Age, sex, weight, height, prior fracture, parental hip fx, smoking, oral glucocorticoids, RA, secondary osteoporosis, alcohol 3+ u/d.
Binary inputs, no # or recency of fractures, no lumbar BMD, no falls, no type 2 diabetes, no TBS in core model.
T4 to L4. Mid-thoracic and thoracolumbar junction are most common fracture sites.
T-score < -1.0 AND one of: women 70+/men 80+; height loss >4cm; undocumented prior vert fx; glucocorticoid 5mg+/d 3+ months.
Grade 0 normal. Grade 1 mild 20-25%. Grade 2 moderate 25-40%. Grade 3 severe >40%.
Much lower dose (4-50 µSv vs ~600 µSv). Point-of-care at time of BMD.

front: Leg elevation for AP spine DXA
back: Flattens lordosis. 60-90 degrees hip/knee flexion typical. Replicate at follow-up.
Iliac crests at L4-L5 disc. Lowest ribs at T12. L3 has longest transverse processes. L5 is flat block.
Exclude vertebra if it differs from adjacent by >1.0 T-score. Also exclude fractures, severe degen change, hardware, laminectomy.
2 minimum. If only 1 evaluable, diagnose from different site. LSC modified for <4.

front: Artifacts that FALSELY ELEVATE spine BMD
back: Osteophytes, facet sclerosis, navel piercing, surgical clips, kyphoplasty cement, barium, iodine contrast.

front: Artifacts that FALSELY LOWER spine BMD
back: Laminectomy, bowel gas overlying a vertebra, scoliosis rotation.
15-20 degrees. Visual: small or hidden lesser trochanter.
Prominent = under-rotated = falsely elevates neck BMD. Small/hidden = correct.
Femoral neck and total hip. Use LOWER T-score. Ward's and greater trochanter NOT for diagnosis.
DIAGNOSIS: lowest T-score of 4 (R/L × neck/total hip). MONITORING: mean bilateral total hip BMD.

front: Which hip ROI has best precision
back: Total hip (LSC 5.0% vs femoral neck 6.9%). Preferred for monitoring. Femoral neck is FRAX input.
Bilateral full-length femur imaging after >=3 years cumulative antiresorptive. Categories: Nondiagnostic/Low/Moderate/High.
Hip/spine cannot be measured or interpreted; hyperparathyroidism; over scanner weight limit.
Non-dominant (reference database). If contraindicated, use contralateral and document.
20 mm segment of radial shaft centered at 1/3 of ulnar styloid-to-olecranon distance.

front: Why forearm is not used for therapy monitoring
back: Mostly cortical bone, BMD change rate too slow to detect.
HIV on specific antiretrovirals; bariatric surgery or >10% weight loss; muscle weakness.
Total fat 3%, total lean 2%, percent fat 2%. Separate from BMD precision study.
Supine centered, arms slightly angled with gap from trunk palms down, legs straight feet vertical, ankles strapped, all metal removed.
Pair SD = |BMD1-BMD2|/sqrt(2). RMS-SD = sqrt(sum SD² / n). RMS-%CV = RMS-SD / mean × 100.
New DXA system. Major software/hardware change. Technologist skill change. Initial after ~100 supervised scans.
LSC = 2.77 × 0.015 = 0.042 g/cm². Change must exceed this for 95% confidence.
Adults 40+, within BMI range, L1-L4 no exclusions. Adjusts FRAX. NOT alone for treatment. NO routine serial TBS monitoring.
DXA (not DEXA). T-score hyphenated. 'White' not 'Caucasian'. 'Hip' not 'femur'. 'Bilateral hips' for both.

front: When to use T-score vs Z-score
back: T-score: postmenopausal women and men 50+. Z-score: premenopausal, men <50, children.
Female white NHANES III age 20-29 for femoral neck/total hip. Uniform white female database regardless of ethnicity.
Lunar spine BMD runs ~0.1 g/cm² HIGHER than Hologic spine. Cannot interchange. Return to same machine.
200 IU intranasal daily. Rarely used. Vertebral fracture only. Cancer safety signal.
Preserves BMD, reduces vertebral/hip/non-vertebral fractures (WHI). Not first-line for osteoporosis alone.
Adult lumbar spine 3-6 minutes. Whole body 10-20 minutes on DPX and DPX-L. Fan beam 10-60 seconds.
Inter-machine LSC must never be applied to patients scanned on a single device. Patients return to same device for follow-up.
National Osteoporosis Foundation rebranded to Bone Health and Osteoporosis Foundation (BHOF) in June 2022.
Mid-L5 (or upper S1) through T12 capturing lowest rib pair. L5 labeled but NOT included in L1-L4 diagnostic sum.
Longer HAL = higher hip fracture risk in postmenopausal women, independent of BMD. Other HSA parameters NOT recommended per ISCD.
Lunar Prodigy 159 kg/350 lb. Lunar iDXA 204 kg/450 lb. Hologic Horizon ~227 kg/500 lb. Norland Elite 283.5 kg/625 lb.
Uniform white female database for T-scores regardless of gender identity/hormones. Diagnose at T <= -2.5 in all TGNC 50+.
Total hip, proximal tibia, and distal femur as soon as medically stable.

front: What uncouples remodeling toward net loss
back: Estrogen deficiency, glucocorticoids, hyperparathyroidism. Antiresorptives preserve; anabolics build.
Nondiagnostic (inadequate), LOW, MODERATE (questionable thickening, warrant radiographs), HIGH (thickening + transverse lucent line, urgent consult).
2022 spec in force through Dec 31, 2026. 2027 spec takes effect Jan 1, 2027. Travis tests against 2022 unless 2027+.
Trabecular, spongy. Opposite of cortical/compact.
Prominent lesser trochanter on hip DXA = insufficient internal rotation. Artifact falsely elevates femoral neck BMD.
